2026-04-21T21:00:32.1632146Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-21T21:00:32.1632745Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-21T21:00:32.1633186Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-21T21:00:32.1633566Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-21T21:00:32.1633885Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-21T21:00:32.1634130Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-21T21:00:32.1634288Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m

2026-04-21T21:00:32.1635178Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-21T21:00:32.1635982Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). 2026-04-21T21:01:51.2627801Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-21T21:01:36.155040315Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58460,\"title\":\"Migrate direct Install-Module calls to use PSModule-Helpers internal feed\",\"body\":\"## Problem\\n\\nSeveral repo-specific scripts directly call `Install-Module` to install PowerShell modules from the public PSGallery, bypassing the centralized `PSModule-Helpers.ps1` helper. \\n\\nThe shared `eng/common/scripts/Helpers/PSModule-Helpers.ps1` has been updated to route all module installs through the internal Azure Artifacts feed (`https://pkgs.dev.azure.com/azure-sdk/public/_packaging/azure-sdk-tools/nuget/v2`) instead of the public PSGallery. This change improves reliability and security by avoiding dependency on external package sources in CI pipelines. The `eng/common` changes (`Verify-Resource-Ref.ps1` and `run-pester-tests.yml`) are already addressed in a separate PR.\\n\\nHowever, the following **repo-specific** scripts still call `Install-Module` directly and will continue installing from the public PSGallery:\\n\\n### Files that need changes\\n\\n| File | Line | Current Code | Module |\\n|---|---|---|---|\\n| `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/eng/scripts/Invoke-SdkRegeneration.ps1` | 54 | `Install-Module -Name powershell-yaml -Force -Scope CurrentUser` | powershell-yaml |\\n| `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/eng/scripts/Invoke-SdkRegeneration.ps1` | 54 | `Install-Module -Name powershell-yaml -Force -Scope CurrentUser` | powershell-yaml |\\n| `eng/scripts/tests/CodeChecks.Tests.ps1` | 12 | `Install-Module Pester -Force` | Pester |\\n\\n## Suggested Fix\\n\\n1. Dot-source `PSModule-Helpers.ps1` at the top of each script (if not already done):\\n   ```powershell\\n   . \\u0026#34;$PSScriptRoot/../../common/scripts/Helpers/PSModule-Helpers.ps1\\u0026#34;\\n   ```\\n   (adjust relative path as needed for each file\\u0026#39;s location)\\n\\n2. Replace direct `Install-Module` calls with the helper function:\\n   - For powershell-yaml: `Install-ModuleIfNotInstalled \\u0026#34;powershell-yaml\\u0026#34; \\u0026#34;0.4.7\\u0026#34; | Import-Module`\\n   - For Pester: `Install-ModuleIfNotInstalled \\u0026#34;Pester\\u0026#34; \\u0026#34;5.3.3\\u0026#34; | Import-Module`\\n\\n## Why This Matters\\n\\n- **Reliability**: The internal feed is more reliable for CI than the public PSGallery, which can have intermittent availability issues.\\n- **Security**: Using an internal feed reduces supply-chain risk from public package repositories.\\n- **Consistency**: All module installs should go through the same centralized helper to ensure uniform behavior across pipelines.\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58460\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"raych1\",\"id\":20296335,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/raych1\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20296335?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"bug\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-21T20:58:58Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-21T20:58:58Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-04-21T21:01:51.2651751Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-21T21:01:36.354987492Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58460,\"title\":\"Migrate direct Install-Module calls to use PSModule-Helpers internal feed\",\"body\":\"## Problem\\n\\nSeveral repo-specific scripts directly call `Install-Module` to install PowerShell modules from the public PSGallery, bypassing the centralized `PSModule-Helpers.ps1` helper. \\n\\nThe shared `eng/common/scripts/Helpers/PSModule-Helpers.ps1` has been updated to route all module installs through the internal Azure Artifacts feed (`https://pkgs.dev.azure.com/azure-sdk/public/_packaging/azure-sdk-tools/nuget/v2`) instead of the public PSGallery. This change improves reliability and security by avoiding dependency on external package sources in CI pipelines. The `eng/common` changes (`Verify-Resource-Ref.ps1` and `run-pester-tests.yml`) are already addressed in a separate PR.\\n\\nHowever, the following **repo-specific** scripts still call `Install-Module` directly and will continue installing from the public PSGallery:\\n\\n### Files that need changes\\n\\n| File | Line | Current Code | Module |\\n|---|---|---|---|\\n| `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/eng/scripts/Invoke-SdkRegeneration.ps1` | 54 | `Install-Module -Name powershell-yaml -Force -Scope CurrentUser` | powershell-yaml |\\n| `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/eng/scripts/Invoke-SdkRegeneration.ps1` | 54 | `Install-Module -Name powershell-yaml -Force -Scope CurrentUser` | powershell-yaml |\\n| `eng/scripts/tests/CodeChecks.Tests.ps1` | 12 | `Install-Module Pester -Force` | Pester |\\n\\n## Suggested Fix\\n\\n1. Dot-source `PSModule-Helpers.ps1` at the top of each script (if not already done):\\n   ```powershell\\n   . \\u0026#34;$PSScriptRoot/../../common/scripts/Helpers/PSModule-Helpers.ps1\\u0026#34;\\n   ```\\n   (adjust relative path as needed for each file\\u0026#39;s location)\\n\\n2. Replace direct `Install-Module` calls with the helper function:\\n   - For powershell-yaml: `Install-ModuleIfNotInstalled \\u0026#34;powershell-yaml\\u0026#34; \\u0026#34;0.4.7\\u0026#34; | Import-Module`\\n   - For Pester: `Install-ModuleIfNotInstalled \\u0026#34;Pester\\u0026#34; \\u0026#34;5.3.3\\u0026#34; | Import-Module`\\n\\n## Why This Matters\\n\\n- **Reliability**: The internal feed is more reliable for CI than the public PSGallery, which can have intermittent availability issues.\\n- **Security**: Using an internal feed reduces supply-chain risk from public package repositories.\\n- **Consistency**: All module installs should go through the same centralized helper to ensure uniform behavior across pipelines.\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58460\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"raych1\",\"id\":20296335,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/raych1\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20296335?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"bug\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-21T20:58:58Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-21T20:58:58Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
